part_title: Section Twenty: Early-Warning Indicators and Outbreak Forecasting
part_subtitle: Early-Warning Indicators and Outbreak Forecasting for Tuta absoluta

items.text: Chapter Ten
Integrated Management of Tuta absoluta
Introduction
An outbreak of Tuta absoluta does not begin when infested fruits appear. It begins earlier, during a period in which several warning signals may pass unnoticed or be interpreted incorrectly.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An outbreak of Tuta absoluta does not begin when infested fruits appear. It begins earlier, during a period in which several warning signals may pass unnoticed or be interpreted incorrectly.

items.text: The first indication of increasing risk may be:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The first indication of increasing risk may be:

items: A gradual increase in male trap catches.
Appearance of eggs along field edges.
An increase in the proportion of eggs close to hatching.
An increase in first- and second-instar larvae.
Beginning of removal of a nearby old tomato field.
A decline in predators after use of a broad-spectrum insecticide.
Repetition of an insecticide group to which susceptibility has declined.
Movement of infestation from leaves to growing points and clusters.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A gradual increase in male trap catches.
[[prepared]]An increase in the proportion of eggs close to hatching.
[[prepared]]An increase in first- and second-instar larvae.
[[prepared]]Beginning of removal of a nearby old tomato field.
[[prepared]]A decline in predators after use of a broad-spectrum insecticide.
[[prepared]]Repetition of an insecticide group to which susceptibility has declined.
[[prepared]]Movement of infestation from leaves to growing points and clusters.

items.text: The function of early warning is to detect these changes before they develop into:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The function of early warning is to detect these changes before they develop into:

items.text: Early warning does not mean certain prediction of the future. It means estimating the probability of increasing infestation on the basis of a set of biological, climatic, spatial, and operational indicators.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Early warning does not mean certain prediction of the future. It means estimating the probability of increasing infestation on the basis of a set of biological, climatic, spatial, and operational indicators.

items.text: The earlier, more accurate, more action-linked, and more verifiable the warning is, the greater its value in preventing an outbreak.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The earlier, more accurate, more action-linked, and more verifiable the warning is, the greater its value in preventing an outbreak.

items.text: Definition of Early Warning

Early warning is an organized system for collecting and analysing a set of data to detect changes that precede an economically important increase in the pest, followed by issuance of an alert specifying:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Early warning is an organized system for collecting and analysing a set of data to detect changes that precede an economically important increase in the pest, followed by issuance of an alert specifying:

items.text: Sending a trap count alone does not constitute an early-warning system because that number does not explain:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Sending a trap count alone does not constitute an early-warning system because that number does not explain:

items: Whether the increase originated inside or outside the field.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Whether the increase originated inside or outside the field.

items.text: Difference Between Monitoring, Early Warning, and Forecasting
Monitoring
Monitoring
Monitoring is the periodic measurement of pest status, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Monitoring is the periodic measurement of pest status, such as:

items.text: It answers the question:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It answers the question:

items.text: What is happening now?
Early Warning
Early Warning
Early warning is the interpretation of current changes that may lead to a near-term increase.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Early warning is the interpretation of current changes that may lead to a near-term increase.

items.text: It answers the question:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It answers the question:

items.text: Are there signs that risk is escalating?
Forecasting
Forecasting
Forecasting uses temperature, population, stage, and immigration-source data to estimate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Forecasting uses temperature, population, stage, and immigration-source data to estimate:

items: The likely period of increasing moth activity.
Probability that infestation will move into fruits.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The likely period of increasing moth activity.
[[prepared]]Probability that infestation will move into fruits.

items.text: It answers the question:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It answers the question:

items.text: What may happen next, and when?
Action Alert
Action Alert
An action alert links the warning to a specific measure, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An action alert links the warning to a specific measure, such as:

items: Notifying recently planted fields that an old field is being removed.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Notifying recently planted fields that an old field is being removed.

items.text: Why Do Early-Warning Systems Fail?
A system may fail because of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A system may fail because of:

items: Use of different lure and trap types.
Failure to link results with temperature.
Issuing a warning without specifying an action.
Delivering the message after larvae have entered fruits.
Absence of a person responsible for response.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Use of different lure and trap types.
[[prepared]]Issuing a warning without specifying an action.
[[prepared]]Delivering the message after larvae have entered fruits.
[[prepared]]Absence of a person responsible for response.

items.text: The value of the system does not lie in collecting the largest possible quantity of data, but in detecting indicators that change the decision before loss occurs.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The value of the system does not lie in collecting the largest possible quantity of data, but in detecting indicators that change the decision before loss occurs.

items.text: Leading and Lagging Indicators
Leading Indicators
Leading Indicators
These appear before economic damage, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These appear before economic damage, such as:

items: Repetition of a material with weak performance.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Repetition of a material with weak performance.

items.text: They allow preventive action.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They allow preventive action.

items.text: Lagging Indicators
Lagging Indicators
These appear after the problem has advanced, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These appear after the problem has advanced, such as:

items.text: They are useful for assessing damage severity but do not always provide sufficient time to prevent it.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They are useful for assessing damage severity but do not always provide sufficient time to prevent it.

items.text: Early warning should therefore rely on leading indicators, while lagging indicators are used to verify its accuracy.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Early warning should therefore rely on leading indicators, while lagging indicators are used to verify its accuracy.

items.text: Layers of the Early-Warning System
The system consists of seven principal layers:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system consists of seven principal layers:

items.text: No single layer is sufficient to issue a reliable warning.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No single layer is sufficient to issue a reliable warning.

items.text: Layer One
Pheromone Traps
Role of Traps in Early Warning
Traps are used to detect:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Traps are used to detect:

items: Differences between field edges and the interior.
Persistence of moths after crop removal.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Differences between field edges and the interior.

items.text: Traps are among the earliest indicators, but they do not directly measure:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Traps are among the earliest indicators, but they do not directly measure:

items.text: They should therefore be used as a gateway for increasing inspection, not as an independent spray order.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They should therefore be used as a gateway for increasing inspection, not as an independent spray order.

items.text: Trap Standardization
For comparisons to be valid, standardize:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]For comparisons to be valid, standardize:

items.text: Changing lure type may increase catches without a genuine increase in the pest population.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Changing lure type may increase catches without a genuine increase in the pest population.

items.text: Converting Catch to a Comparable Unit
When trap operating periods differ, calculate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When trap operating periods differ, calculate:

items.text: Mean daily catch = Total males captured ÷ Actual number of operating days
Example:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Example:

items: A trap captured 35 males over 7 days.

items.text: Exclude days during which the trap was:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Exclude days during which the trap was:

items.text: Analysis of Trap Trends
The current reading should not be interpreted alone; it should be compared with a time series.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The current reading should not be interpreted alone; it should be compared with a time series.

items.text: Trends may be classified as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Trends may be classified as:

items.text: Stable Low Trend
No clear increase across readings.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No clear increase across readings.

items.text: Single Increase
An increase in one round that may result from temporary immigration, a lure change, or weather.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An increase in one round that may result from temporary immigration, a lure change, or weather.

items.text: Consecutive Increase
An increase over two or more rounds, whose importance rises when accompanied by fresh eggs.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An increase over two or more rounds, whose importance rises when accompanied by fresh eggs.

items.text: Peak Followed by Decline
This may represent a flight wave, and eggs and hatching arising from it should be investigated.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may represent a flight wave, and eggs and hatching arising from it should be investigated.

items.text: Continued Increase Despite Intervention
This may indicate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may indicate:

items.text: Edge-to-Interior Trap Ratio
A descriptive indicator may be calculated:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A descriptive indicator may be calculated:

items.text: Edge-to-interior ratio = Mean catch in edge traps ÷ Mean catch in interior traps
When the ratio is clearly high and a nearby external source is present, external immigration becomes more likely.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When the ratio is clearly high and a nearby external source is present, external immigration becomes more likely.

items.text: When interior traps increase and several life stages are distributed throughout the field, internal reproduction becomes more likely.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When interior traps increase and several life stages are distributed throughout the field, internal reproduction becomes more likely.

items.text: The ratio is not a fixed threshold. It is a comparative tool for the same field over time.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The ratio is not a fixed threshold. It is a comparative tool for the same field over time.

items.text: Warning Signals from Traps
Catch becomes more important when combined with one or more of the following:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Catch becomes more important when combined with one or more of the following:

items: Beginning of removal of a neighbouring field.
Concentration of catches along one edge.
Presence of an attractive young crop.
A previous history of immigration waves during the same period.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Beginning of removal of a neighbouring field.
[[prepared]]A previous history of immigration waves during the same period.

items.text: A high trap catch without plant evidence should trigger an inspection alert, not a spray alert.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A high trap catch without plant evidence should trigger an inspection alert, not a spray alert.

items.text: Layer Two
Egg Indicators
Importance of Eggs
Eggs are the closest direct indicator that a new generation is becoming established in the field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Eggs are the closest direct indicator that a new generation is becoming established in the field.

items.text: Their detection allows:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Their detection allows:

items: Timing of Bt or another early-stage agent.
Containment of the edge before larvae spread.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Timing of Bt or another early-stage agent.
[[prepared]]Containment of the edge before larvae spread.

items.text: Early warning should focus on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Early warning should focus on:

items.text: Percentage of Plants Carrying Eggs
Calculate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Calculate:

items.text: Plants carrying eggs (%) = Number of plants on which eggs were found ÷ Total plants inspected × 100
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Plants carrying eggs (%) = Number of plants on which eggs were found ÷ Total plants inspected × 100

items.text: This indicator measures the distribution of oviposition, not only egg density.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This indicator measures the distribution of oviposition, not only egg density.

items.text: There may be:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]There may be:

items: High density on a few plants.
Or low density distributed over a large area.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Or low density distributed over a large area.

items.text: The response differs between these situations.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The response differs between these situations.

items.text: Egg Density
This may be expressed as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may be expressed as:

items: Eggs per fixed number of leaflets.

items.text: The unit should remain constant among inspection rounds.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The unit should remain constant among inspection rounds.

items.text: Data collected from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Data collected from:

items.text: Should not be compared directly.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Should not be compared directly.

items.text: Egg Age as a Temporal Indicator
Eggs should be classified as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Eggs should be classified as:

items.text: This classification is important because:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This classification is important because:

items: Fresh eggs provide more time for release or preparation.
Eggs close to hatching indicate that the response window is narrowing.
Hatched eggshells indicate larvae have already begun to emerge.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Fresh eggs provide more time for release or preparation.
[[prepared]]Eggs close to hatching indicate that the response window is narrowing.
[[prepared]]Hatched eggshells indicate larvae have already begun to emerge.

items.text: Recording only “eggs present” without identifying their status is insufficient.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Recording only “eggs present” without identifying their status is insufficient.

items.text: Live Unparasitized Eggs
An approximate indicator may be calculated:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An approximate indicator may be calculated:

items.text: Eggs at risk of hatching = Total live eggs − Parasitized or dead eggs
This number represents a more direct risk than the raw total number of eggs.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This number represents a more direct risk than the raw total number of eggs.

items.text: However, consider:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]However, consider:

items: Difficulty of visually detecting early parasitism.
Variation in timing of colour change.

items.text: When uncertain, retain samples for rearing or magnified examination.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When uncertain, retain samples for rearing or magnified examination.

items.text: Egg Distribution Along Field Edges
Field studies have shown that Tuta absoluta eggs may have an aggregated distribution and that high densities may occur along crop edges.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Field studies have shown that Tuta absoluta eggs may have an aggregated distribution and that high densities may occur along crop edges.

items.text: An increase in eggs along one edge warns of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An increase in eggs along one edge warns of:

items.text: Inspection should be increased:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection should be increased:

items: In the interior to confirm the extent of spread.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]In the interior to confirm the extent of spread.

items.text: Layer Three
Mines and Larval Instars
New Mines as a Warning
Appearance of the first new mines indicates that:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Appearance of the first new mines indicates that:

items: The opportunity to target eggs has partly passed.
The programme requires rapid intervention or assessment.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The opportunity to target eggs has partly passed.
[[prepared]]The programme requires rapid intervention or assessment.

items.text: New mines should not be confused with old mines.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]New mines should not be confused with old mines.

items.text: Percentage of Plants with Active Mines
Calculate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Calculate:

items.text: Plants with active mines (%) = Number of plants carrying at least one active mine ÷ Total plants inspected × 100
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Plants with active mines (%) = Number of plants carrying at least one active mine ÷ Total plants inspected × 100

items.text: This indicator measures the spread of infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This indicator measures the spread of infestation.

items.text: Also record:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Also record:

items: Number of active mines per plant.

items.text: Dominance of L1 and L2
An increasing proportion of early instars warns of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An increasing proportion of early instars warns of:

items: An intervention opportunity that is still available.
A likely rapid increase in mines over the following days.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]An intervention opportunity that is still available.
[[prepared]]A likely rapid increase in mines over the following days.

items.text: The response should occur before they develop into:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The response should occur before they develop into:

items.text: The warning value is greater under rising temperatures.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The warning value is greater under rising temperatures.

items.text: Appearance of L3 and L4
This is not an early-warning indicator in the strict sense. It shows that part of the preventive opportunity has already been lost.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This is not an early-warning indicator in the strict sense. It shows that part of the preventive opportunity has already been lost.

items.text: It indicates:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It indicates:

items.text: The field should be escalated from monitoring to corrective response.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The field should be escalated from monitoring to corrective response.

items.text: Rate of New-Mine Appearance
A group of plants or leaves can be marked and reinspected.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A group of plants or leaves can be marked and reinspected.

items.text: Calculate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Calculate:

items.text: Rate of new-mine appearance = Number of mines appearing since the previous round ÷ Number of plants or days
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Rate of new-mine appearance = Number of mines appearing since the previous round ÷ Number of plants or days

items.text: This is more informative than the cumulative total number of mines.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This is more informative than the cumulative total number of mines.

items.text: It helps determine:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It helps determine:

items.text: Layer Four
Growing Points, Clusters, and Fruits
Growing-Point Infestation as an Escalation Signal
Appearance of mines or larvae in growing points indicates:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Appearance of mines or larvae in growing points indicates:

items: Proximity of larvae to flowers and clusters.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Proximity of larvae to flowers and clusters.

items.text: The indicator becomes more serious in:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The indicator becomes more serious in:

items.text: Infestation Approaching Clusters
Eggs or mines on leaves touching clusters are a stronger warning signal than those on distant lower leaves.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Eggs or mines on leaves touching clusters are a stronger warning signal than those on distant lower leaves.

items.text: Risk signs include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Risk signs include:

items: Larvae in leaves adjacent to fruits.
Initial holes in flowers or sepals.
Larval movement between a leaf and a cluster.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Larval movement between a leaf and a cluster.

items.text: Inspection frequency should be increased without waiting for large holes to appear in fruits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection frequency should be increased without waiting for large holes to appear in fruits.

items.text: First Infested Fruit
The first confirmed infested fruit marks a transition from warning to actual economic damage.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The first confirmed infested fruit marks a transition from warning to actual economic damage.

items.text: Immediately:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Immediately:

items: Determine whether live larvae are present.

items.text: One fruit is not evidence of a general outbreak, but it should not be ignored.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]One fruit is not evidence of a general outbreak, but it should not be ignored.

items.text: Increase in Infested Fruits Between Rounds
Calculate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Calculate:

items.text: Rate of increase in fruit infestation = Current infestation percentage − Previous infestation percentage
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Rate of increase in fruit infestation = Current infestation percentage − Previous infestation percentage

items.text: When the rate increases over two rounds, this indicates that:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When the rate increases over two rounds, this indicates that:

items: The previous intervention did not stop infestation.
Leaf inspection may not fully represent the risk.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The previous intervention did not stop infestation.
[[prepared]]Leaf inspection may not fully represent the risk.

items.text: The response level should be escalated even if trap catch declines.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The response level should be escalated even if trap catch declines.

items.text: Layer Five
Natural Enemies
Natural Enemies as Part of Early Warning
An early-warning system should not measure the pest alone because outbreak probability is influenced by the capacity of natural enemies to slow population growth.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An early-warning system should not measure the pest alone because outbreak probability is influenced by the capacity of natural enemies to slow population growth.

items.text: Monitor:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Monitor:

items: Effects of insecticides on these organisms.

items.text: An increase in eggs may be less serious when accompanied by strong, stable biological regulation, whereas a limited increase may be more serious when beneficial organisms have collapsed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An increase in eggs may be less serious when accompanied by strong, stable biological regulation, whereas a limited increase may be more serious when beneficial organisms have collapsed.

items.text: Decline in Parasitism Percentage
A decline in parasitism becomes a warning when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A decline in parasitism becomes a warning when:

items: It was high and then fell suddenly.
Parasitoids failed to emerge from release cards.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]It was high and then fell suddenly.
[[prepared]]Parasitoids failed to emerge from release cards.

items.text: Investigate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Investigate:

items: Release timing relative to egg age.

items.text: Absolute Number of Surviving Eggs
Parasitism percentage alone is insufficient.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Parasitism percentage alone is insufficient.

items.text: Example:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Example:

items.text: If there are:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]If there are:

items.text: Approximately 6 eggs remain unparasitized.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Approximately 6 eggs remain unparasitized.

items.text: If there are:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]If there are:

items.text: Approximately 1.2 eggs remain unparasitized.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Approximately 1.2 eggs remain unparasitized.

items.text: The first situation may therefore be more serious despite the higher parasitism percentage.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The first situation may therefore be more serious despite the higher parasitism percentage.

items.text: Decline in Predator Abundance
A decline in predators is a warning when it coincides with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A decline in predators is a warning when it coincides with:

items.text: Distinguish between:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Distinguish between:

items: A temporary decline due to movement.
A genuine collapse indicated by absence of nymphs and reproduction.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A genuine collapse indicated by absence of nymphs and reproduction.

items.text: Pest-to-Predator Ratio
An internal descriptive indicator may compare:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An internal descriptive indicator may compare:

items.text: There is no universal fixed ratio for Tuta absoluta that applies to all species and conditions.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]There is no universal fixed ratio for Tuta absoluta that applies to all species and conditions.

items.text: However, rapid pest increase while predator density remains stable or declines indicates weakening natural regulation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]However, rapid pest increase while predator density remains stable or declines indicates weakening natural regulation.

items.text: Layer Six
Climatic and Thermal Data
Temperature as a Principal Driver of Development
Temperature affects:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Temperature affects:

items.text: Temperature alone should not be used to predict damage because population dynamics are also influenced by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Temperature alone should not be used to predict damage because population dynamics are also influenced by:

items.text: Degree-Days
Degree-Days
Degree-days measure accumulated heat above the lower developmental threshold.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Degree-days measure accumulated heat above the lower developmental threshold.

items.text: In the simplest form:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In the simplest form:

items.text: Daily degree-days = Mean daily temperature − Lower developmental threshold
Provided that the mean temperature lies within the developmental range.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Provided that the mean temperature lies within the developmental range.

items.text: More accurate calculations may use:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]More accurate calculations may use:

items.text: One consistent, locally validated model should be used.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]One consistent, locally validated model should be used.

items.text: Variation in Published Thermal Constants
Studies have produced different values for developmental thresholds and thermal constants according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Studies have produced different values for developmental thresholds and thermal constants according to:

items.text: Therefore, one should not combine:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Therefore, one should not combine:

items: And a thermal constant from another.

items.text: The model should be used as an integrated unit.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The model should be used as an integrated unit.

items.text: Using Temperature to Forecast Hatching
Forecasting may begin from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Forecasting may begin from:

items: Or detection of a fresh egg cohort.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Or detection of a fresh egg cohort.

items.text: Thermal accumulation is then used to estimate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Thermal accumulation is then used to estimate:

items.text: Sex-pheromone traps do not precisely identify oviposition date. Hatching forecasts are therefore more accurate when temperature is linked to eggs observed on the plant.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Sex-pheromone traps do not precisely identify oviposition date. Hatching forecasts are therefore more accurate when temperature is linked to eggs observed on the plant.

items.text: Rising Temperature as a Signal to Shorten the Interval
When temperature rises over several days:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When temperature rises over several days:

items: Do not wait for the traditional weekly visit.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Do not wait for the traditional weekly visit.

items.text: A change in developmental speed is itself a warning, even before pest density changes.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A change in developmental speed is itself a warning, even before pest density changes.

items.text: Cold Does Not Eliminate Risk
When temperature declines:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When temperature declines:

items.text: Eggs and larvae may nevertheless continue, and a later increase in temperature may produce a synchronized development wave.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Eggs and larvae may nevertheless continue, and a later increase in temperature may produce a synchronized development wave.

items.text: Monitoring should therefore not stop during cool periods.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Monitoring should therefore not stop during cool periods.

items.text: Actual Temperature Within the Canopy
Temperatures at:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Temperatures at:

items.text: May differ from weather-station temperature.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]May differ from weather-station temperature.

items.text: Models should therefore be interpreted cautiously, especially in:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Models should therefore be interpreted cautiously, especially in:

items.text: A weather station as close to the field as possible should be used.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A weather station as close to the field as possible should be used.

items.text: Humidity, Rainfall, and Wind
Humidity affects:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Humidity affects:

items.text: Wind affects:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Wind affects:

items.text: Rainfall or overhead irrigation may affect:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Rainfall or overhead irrigation may affect:

items.text: These factors should be integrated with temperature rather than used separately.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These factors should be integrated with temperature rather than used separately.

items.text: Layer Seven
Source Fields and the Agricultural Landscape
An Old Field as an Early-Warning Indicator
An old field becomes a risk source when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An old field becomes a risk source when:

items: It approaches the end of harvest.

items.text: The expected date of each operation should be recorded because immigration may begin before complete removal.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The expected date of each operation should be recorded because immigration may begin before complete removal.

items.text: Calendar of Surrounding Fields
The warning map should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The warning map should include:

items: Field position relative to prevailing wind.

items.text: A change in a neighbouring field from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A change in a neighbouring field from:

items.text: Active production → Final harvest → Irrigation cessation → Removal
Represents a sequence of risk signals for recently planted fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Represents a sequence of risk signals for recently planted fields.

items.text: Nurseries and Sorting Stations
The warning level should be raised near:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The warning level should be raised near:

items: A sorting station where rejected fruits accumulate.
A factory that fails to manage waste.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A sorting station where rejected fruits accumulate.
[[prepared]]A factory that fails to manage waste.

items.text: Monitoring traps should be installed and surrounding plants inspected at these sites.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Monitoring traps should be installed and surrounding plants inspected at these sites.

items.text: Alternative Hosts
Increasing infestation in:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Increasing infestation in:

items.text: May warn of risk to nearby tomato crops.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]May warn of risk to nearby tomato crops.

items.text: However, it should be confirmed that the host supports completion of the pest life cycle rather than merely carrying transient individuals.

Decline in Monitoring Quality
An administrative alert should be issued when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An administrative alert should be issued when:

items: Old and new mines are confused.
Data arrive after the decision deadline.

items.text: Absence of data is itself a risk factor because infestation may develop undetected.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Absence of data is itself a risk factor because infestation may develop undetected.

items.text: Approaching Lure Expiry
The installation date of every lure should be recorded.

items.text: Before its effective period ends:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Before its effective period ends:

items: Replace it without a monitoring gap.
Avoid changing all lure types mid-series without documentation.
Do not interpret a decline caused by a weak lure as a decline in the pest.

[[prepared]]Do not interpret a decline caused by a weak lure as a decline in the pest.

The pattern recurs in more than one sector.

Warning-Level System
Green Level
Indicators:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Indicators:

items: Absence of a substantial live egg population.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Absence of a substantial live egg population.

items.text: Action:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items: Do not implement an unjustified intervention.

items.text: Yellow Level
Possible indicators:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Possible indicators:

items: A single increase or the beginning of an upward trap trend.
Presence of a nearby old field.
Failure in part of the monitoring network.

items.text: Action:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Indicators:

items: Apply a biological, microbial, or localized intervention.

Indicators:

Indicators:

items: High densities of several life stages.
Proximity to harvest with limited options.
Transformation of the field into a source.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Action:

items: Evaluate advancing harvest or terminating the sector.

Experimental Scoring System
An internal index may be developed to rank field priority.

items.text: It should not be used alone to issue a spray decision and should not be adopted before local calibration.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It should not be used alone to issue a spray decision and should not be adopted before local calibration.

items.text: Change-Based Warning Rather Than Absolute Numbers
In some fields, baseline trap catch is relatively high while plant damage remains low.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In some fields, baseline trap catch is relatively high while plant damage remains low.

items.text: Therefore:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Therefore:

items.text: Weekly rate of change
May be more useful than the absolute count.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]May be more useful than the absolute count.

items.text: Calculate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Calculate:

items.text: Relative change (%) = [(Current reading − Previous reading) ÷ Previous reading] × 100
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Relative change (%) = [(Current reading − Previous reading) ÷ Previous reading] × 100

items.text: When the previous reading is zero or very low, misleading mathematical interpretation should be avoided. Use the actual number and field context.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When the previous reading is zero or very low, misleading mathematical interpretation should be avoided. Use the actual number and field context.

The rate of increase may be compared between periods.

This aims to predict what may occur over the following days, such as:

items.text: Thermal Models Do Not Calculate Immigration
A thermal model may forecast development of locally present individuals, but it cannot by itself predict:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A thermal model may forecast development of locally present individuals, but it cannot by itself predict:

items: Entry of moths from a distant field.

items.text: Weekly Warning Bulletin
The bulletin should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The bulletin should include:

items.text: It should not contain an automatic recommendation for one insecticide across all fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It should not contain an automatic recommendation for one insecticide across all fields.

items.text: Example of a Yellow Alert
Situation: A limited increase in male catch along the northern edge coinciding with the beginning of removal of a nearby old tomato field, with no confirmed active mines to date.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Situation: A limited increase in male catch along the northern edge coinciding with the beginning of removal of a nearby old tomato field, with no confirmed active mines to date.

items.text: Action:

items: Inspect eggs along the northern edge.
Recheck traps after a shorter interval.
Do not spray on the basis of catch alone.
Notify the team when eggs or hatching are detected.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Do not spray on the basis of catch alone.
[[prepared]]Notify the team when eggs or hatching are detected.

items.text: Example of an Orange Alert

Situation: Consecutive increases in trap catches, appearance of fresh eggs and initial L1 hatching along the eastern and northern edges, together with a decline in parasitism compared with the previous round.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Situation: Consecutive increases in trap catches, appearance of fresh eggs and initial L1 hatching along the eastern and northern edges, together with a decline in parasitism compared with the previous round.

items.text: Action:

items: Intensify inspection of edges and the following inner rows.
Apply a compatible early intervention targeting hatching when decision criteria are met.
Reassess before larvae progress to advanced instars.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Intensify inspection of edges and the following inner rows.
[[prepared]]Apply a compatible early intervention targeting hatching when decision criteria are met.
[[prepared]]Reassess before larvae progress to advanced instars.

items.text: Example of a Red Alert
Situation: L2 and L3 are distributed in more than one sector, the first infested fruits have appeared, and immigration from an old field is continuing.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Situation: L2 and L3 are distributed in more than one sector, the first infested fruits have appeared, and immigration from an old field is continuing.

items.text: Action:

items: Apply a selective corrective intervention according to the IRAC record and preharvest interval.
Coordinate termination of the source field.
Assess the result within a short period.
Collect samples if larvae persist in well-covered areas.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Apply a selective corrective intervention according to the IRAC record and preharvest interval.
[[prepared]]Assess the result within a short period.
[[prepared]]Collect samples if larvae persist in well-covered areas.

items.text: Warning Confidence
Warnings should be classified as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Warnings should be classified as:

items.text: High Confidence
When the following agree:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When the following agree:

items.text: Moderate Confidence
When two or three indicators are present with some uncertainty.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When two or three indicators are present with some uncertainty.

items.text: Low Confidence
When based on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When based on:

items.text: The bulletin should state the confidence level so that the forecast is not treated as certain fact.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The bulletin should state the confidence level so that the forecast is not treated as certain fact.

The system should define:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system should define:

items.text: A weekly bulletin may lose value during hot periods if collection and analysis take several days.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A weekly bulletin may lose value during hot periods if collection and analysis take several days.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A simple system used consistently is better than a complex system whose data are not updated.

items.text: Field Validation of the Forecast
After issuing a warning, review:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]After issuing a warning, review:

items: Did hatching occur at the expected time?
Did infestation move into the field interior?
Did trap catches reach a new peak?
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Did hatching occur at the expected time?
[[prepared]]Did infestation move into the field interior?
[[prepared]]Did trap catches reach a new peak?

items.text: Record the result as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Record the result as:

items.text: Correct forecast – Forecast too early – Forecast too late – False alarm
Then adjust the model.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Then adjust the model.

items.text: False Alarm
A false alarm is a high-level warning without occurrence of the expected risk.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A false alarm is a high-level warning without occurrence of the expected risk.

items.text: It may result from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It may result from:

items: A moth wave that did not result in oviposition.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A moth wave that did not result in oviposition.

items.text: A false alarm does not mean monitoring is useless, but it requires improvement of interpretation rules.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A false alarm does not mean monitoring is useless, but it requires improvement of interpretation rules.

items.text: Late Warning
This occurs when the warning is issued after:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This occurs when the warning is issued after:

items.text: Causes include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Causes include:

items: Failure to adjust intervals as temperature rises.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Failure to adjust intervals as temperature rises.

items.text: A late warning is more serious than a false alarm because it removes the opportunity for prevention.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A late warning is more serious than a false alarm because it removes the opportunity for prevention.

items.text: Measuring Early-Warning System Performance
Use indicators including:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Use indicators including:

items: Percentage of outbreaks preceded by a warning.
Mean number of days between warning and appearance of damage.
Number of cases detected only after fruit infestation.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Percentage of outbreaks preceded by a warning.
[[prepared]]Mean number of days between warning and appearance of damage.
[[prepared]]Number of cases detected only after fruit infestation.

items.text: Sensitivity and Specificity of the Warning System
Sensitivity
The system's ability to detect cases that will develop into genuine risk.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system's ability to detect cases that will develop into genuine risk.

items.text: Specificity
Its ability to avoid issuing a high-level warning when no genuine risk exists.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Its ability to avoid issuing a high-level warning when no genuine risk exists.

items.text: A highly sensitive system may generate many false alarms, whereas an overly conservative system may miss outbreaks.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A highly sensitive system may generate many false alarms, whereas an overly conservative system may miss outbreaks.

items.text: A balance should be achieved according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A balance should be achieved according to:

items.text: Adapting Early Warning for Processing Tomatoes
Greater weight should be given to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Greater weight should be given to:

items: Presence of large old processing-tomato fields.

items.text: The warning should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The warning should include:

items.text: Adapting Early Warning for Fresh-Market Tomatoes
Greater weight should be given to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Greater weight should be given to:

items: Preharvest interval before the next picking.

items.text: Appearance of a limited number of infested fruits may raise the warning level more rapidly than in processing tomatoes, depending on market specifications.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Appearance of a limited number of infested fruits may raise the warning level more rapidly than in processing tomatoes, depending on market specifications.

items.text: Application Under Egyptian Conditions
The Egyptian early-warning system should be divided according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The Egyptian early-warning system should be divided according to:

items: Winter, spring, summer, and autumn production cycles.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Winter, spring, summer, and autumn production cycles.

items.text: One time window for forecasting hatching should not be used across all regions.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]One time window for forecasting hatching should not be used across all regions.

items.text: Each region should be linked to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each region should be linked to:

items.text: Simplified Model for an Egyptian Field
The following data should be collected weekly:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following data should be collected weekly:

rows: Percentage of plants with active mines

items.text: Common Errors
Error One: Treating the Trap as a Complete Early-Warning System
It is only one layer.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It is only one layer.

items.text: Error Two: Establishing a Universal Spray Threshold from Trap Catch
The relationship between catch and damage differs among situations.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The relationship between catch and damage differs among situations.

items.text: Error Three: Comparing Different Lures
The difference may reflect attraction strength rather than pest density.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The difference may reflect attraction strength rather than pest density.

items.text: Error Four: Ignoring Eggs
This removes the most important intervention window.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This removes the most important intervention window.

items.text: Error Five: Recording Egg Presence Without Egg Age
This does not show how much response time remains.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This does not show how much response time remains.

items.text: Error Six: Relying on Total Mine Counts
New and active mines should be measured.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]New and active mines should be measured.

items.text: Error Seven: Ignoring Natural Enemies
This may overestimate or underestimate risk.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may overestimate or underestimate risk.

items.text: Error Eight: Using an Unvalidated Non-Local Thermal Model
This may incorrectly predict stage timing.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may incorrectly predict stage timing.

items.text: Error Nine: Combining a Threshold from One Study with a Thermal Constant from Another
This produces an internally inconsistent model.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This produces an internally inconsistent model.

items.text: Error Ten: Failing to Include Old Fields
Forecasts for an isolated field become unreliable.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Forecasts for an isolated field become unreliable.

items.text: Error Eleven: Issuing a Warning Without an Action
This does not change the outcome.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This does not change the outcome.

items.text: Error Twelve: Delaying the Message
It may arrive after hatching or fruit infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It may arrive after hatching or fruit infestation.

items.text: Error Thirteen: Using a Point Total as an Automatic Spray Order
Technical interpretation remains necessary.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Technical interpretation remains necessary.

items.text: Error Fourteen: Failing to Assess Forecast Accuracy
This prevents system improvement.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This prevents system improvement.

items.text: Error Fifteen: Relying on the regional average and concealing hotspots
Risk locations should be identified.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Risk locations should be identified.

items.text: Research and Applied Priorities in Egypt

items: 1. Standardize trap and lure types within monitoring networks.
2. Develop catch baselines for each region and production cycle.
3. Link catch data with eggs, larvae, and fruit infestation.
4. Develop a locally validated thermal model.
5. Use weather stations close to fields.
6. Develop maps of old and newly planted fields.
7. Identify early indicators specific to processing tomatoes.
8. Develop separate indicators for fresh-market tomatoes.
9. Study egg distribution along Egyptian field edges.
10. Measure the effect of wind direction.
11. Include parasitism percentage in the warning dashboard.
13. Establish a database of IRAC groups.
14. Link warnings with removal of old fields.
15. Develop a simple Arabic-language application for engineers.
16. Measure the sensitivity and specificity of warning levels.
18. Train monitoring teams to identify egg and larval age.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Standardize trap and lure types within monitoring networks.
[[prepared]]2. Develop catch baselines for each region and production cycle.
[[prepared]]3. Link catch data with eggs, larvae, and fruit infestation.
[[prepared]]4. Develop a locally validated thermal model.
[[prepared]]5. Use weather stations close to fields.
[[prepared]]6. Develop maps of old and newly planted fields.
[[prepared]]7. Identify early indicators specific to processing tomatoes.
[[prepared]]8. Develop separate indicators for fresh-market tomatoes.
[[prepared]]9. Study egg distribution along Egyptian field edges.
[[prepared]]10. Measure the effect of wind direction.
[[prepared]]11. Include parasitism percentage in the warning dashboard.
[[prepared]]13. Establish a database of IRAC groups.
[[prepared]]14. Link warnings with removal of old fields.
[[prepared]]15. Develop a simple Arabic-language application for engineers.
[[prepared]]16. Measure the sensitivity and specificity of warning levels.
[[prepared]]18. Train monitoring teams to identify egg and larval age.

items.text: Scientific Summary of Section Twenty
Early warning for Tuta absoluta does not depend on a single trap count. It depends on integrating a set of leading indicators, including:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Early warning for Tuta absoluta does not depend on a single trap count. It depends on integrating a set of leading indicators, including:

items.text: Traps are used to detect male activity and flight waves, but they do not directly measure oviposition or fruit infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Traps are used to detect male activity and flight waves, but they do not directly measure oviposition or fruit infestation.

items.text: Eggs are the most important direct indicator that a new generation is becoming established. They should be classified by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Eggs are the most important direct indicator that a new generation is becoming established. They should be classified by:

items.text: New mines and presence of L1 and L2 also indicate a hatching wave requiring a response before larvae advance.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]New mines and presence of L1 and L2 also indicate a hatching wave requiring a response before larvae advance.

items.text: Appearance of L3 and L4 and fruit infestation are lagging indicators showing that part of the preventive opportunity has been lost.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Appearance of L3 and L4 and fruit infestation are lagging indicators showing that part of the preventive opportunity has been lost.

items.text: Temperature affects the time available for response. Inspection intervals should therefore be shortened when development accelerates rather than remaining fixed at weekly intervals.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Temperature affects the time available for response. Inspection intervals should therefore be shortened when development accelerates rather than remaining fixed at weekly intervals.

items.text: Degree-days can help estimate hatching and generation timing, but they do not independently forecast immigration or movement of transplants and residues.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Degree-days can help estimate hatching and generation timing, but they do not independently forecast immigration or movement of transplants and residues.

items.text: Old fields, sorting stations, nurseries, and alternative hosts should be included in the warning map.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Old fields, sorting stations, nurseries, and [[term:alternative-host]]alternative hosts[[/term]] should be included in the warning map.

items.text: The operational system consists of five levels:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The operational system consists of five levels:

items: Critical for containment and assessment of crop continuation.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Critical for containment and assessment of crop continuation.

items.text: A warning is useful only when it specifies:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A warning is useful only when it specifies:

items.text: The system is effective when it provides sufficient time to target eggs and young larvae, reduces fruit infestation and preventive sprays, and prevents old fields from reinvading newly planted crops.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system is effective when it provides sufficient time to target eggs and young larvae, reduces fruit infestation and preventive sprays, and prevents old fields from reinvading newly planted crops.
